title: Avatar Accessory Duel: Sunglasses Showdown
description: Two readyplayer.me avatars face off in a turn-based accessory duel arena. The player must equip, position, scale, and tint sunglasses on their avatar while managing a limited energy budget (conservation law). Each action costs energy, and players can undo/redo moves to optimize their style score before locking in each round.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (importmap from cdn.jsdelivr.net) that loads readyplayer.me.glb twice (player avatar on the left, opponent on the right) and SunglassesKhronos.glb as an equippable accessory. Set up a PerspectiveCamera at (0,1.5,3) looking at origin, with ambient light (0.5) and a directional light from upper-right. Use a flat circular arena floor with a subtle grid texture. Implement a turn-based duel with these interactions: (1) Click 'Equip' button to attach sunglasses to the player avatar's head bone via skeleton traversal. (2) Use W/S keys to nudge sunglasses up/down on the face (position.y ±0.005 per press). (3) Use A/D keys to tilt sunglasses left/right (rotation.z ±2 degrees). (4) Use Q/E keys to scale sunglasses up/down (±0.05). (5) Click color swatches (red, gold, blue, black) to tint the sunglasses material. (6) Each action costs 1 energy from a budget of 14 energy points per round (conservation law — energy is displayed as a bar, total actions across all categories cannot exceed budget). (7) Implement Undo (Ctrl+Z) and Redo (Ctrl+Y) that restore previous transform/color states and refund/re-deduct energy. (8) A 'Lock In' button finalizes the round, computes a style score based on position symmetry, tilt creativity, scale appropriateness, and color rarity. (9) The opponent avatar automatically gets random sunglasses placement for comparison. (10) After 3 rounds, display total score. Show an HTML overlay HUD with energy bar, round counter, score, undo/redo buttons, color swatches, and equip/lock-in buttons. Use OrbitControls (limited vertical) so the player can inspect both avatars. Store full action history as a stack of state snapshots for undo/redo. Animate a subtle idle bounce on both avatars using sinusoidal position.y offset.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  sunglassesEquipped: boolean,         // whether sunglasses are attached to player avatar
  sunglassesPositionY: number,         // vertical offset of sunglasses on face
  sunglassesRotationZ: number,         // tilt angle of sunglasses in radians
  sunglassesScale: number,         // uniform scale factor of sunglasses
  sunglassesTintHex: number,         // current color tint as hex integer
  energyRemaining: number,         // energy points left in current round (0-14)
  undoStackDepth: number,         // count of undo-able actions in history
  redoStackDepth: number,         // count of redo-able actions in history
  roundLocked: boolean,         // whether player has finalized their turn
  totalActionsThisRound: number,         // cumulative action count for budget enforcement
}
